strategic_alpha.reasoning: Real infrastructure gap — agents need email and no prior solution was agent-native. $6M seed + YC validates market. MCP server shows framework-thinking. Differentiated from generic SMTP tools.

execution_grit.reasoning: Public API docs, free tier testable without enterprise gate, real-time events verifiable via webhook test. SOC 2 Type II is credible trust signal. Claims concrete and checkable.

craft_and_soul.reasoning: Developer-first product with clean API design. agentmail.to docs site likely well-structured (YC-backed teams usually execute on DX). No fluff — functional focus.

value_signal.reasoning: Free tier with 3 inboxes is genuine entry point. $20/mo for 10 inboxes is fair for infra service. Pricing is public and transparent — good score.

claims.claim: Homepage publicly accessible and value proposition clearly stated
claims.evidence: AgentMail is the email inbox API for AI agents.

claims.claim: Free tier (3 inboxes) confirmed without login required for pricing page
API documentation publicly accessible at docs.agentmail.to
claims.evidence: The homepage includes a link labeled 'DOCS' which suggests the presence of documentation.

claims.claim: Pricing page exists with transparent tier breakdown
Real-time webhook/websocket feature documented with example
SOC 2 Type II certification claimed publicly
Framework integrations listed (LangChain, CrewAI, LlamaIndex, LiveKit)
